fact: April 30 is exactly 120 days into the year - that's one-third of the whole year gone!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when many trees are exploding with fresh green leaves after being bare all winter.
Why do so many baby animals show up right now? Because spring warmth makes food easier to find for hungry new families!
The sun rises about 6 minutes earlier than it did just one week ago in most places - that's like getting a free snooze button every day.
April 30 falls smack in the middle of Taurus season, when the zodiac bull is charging through the sky.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the heart of autumn when leaves turn brilliant orange and red.
Gross but cool: This is prime time for earthworms to come wiggling up after spring rains, making sidewalks look like spaghetti!
The moon can be in any phase on April 30, but when it's full around this time, it's called the Pink Moon - even though it's not actually pink.
In northern places like Alaska, the sun stays up for nearly 19 hours on this date - imagine having dinner in broad daylight at 10 PM!
April 30 sits exactly 54 days after the spring equinox, when day and night were perfectly equal length.
Butterflies are having their biggest party of the year right now in temperate regions - monarchs, swallowtails, and painted ladies are all fluttering around.
The air temperature typically swings wildly on this date - it could be 40°F in the morning and 70°F by afternoon in many places.
